title: Aldama subdentata
intro: Aldama subdentata, also known as Guaco or Huaco, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aldama subdentata include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and it is also used in the form of capsules or tablets.

This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics, such as its flowers and leaves, which are similar to those of other Rosaceae species. Within the Rosaceae family, Aldama subdentata is also classified in the subfamily Rosoideae.
